run_summary: Portfolio is cash-heavy after recent exit activity. New flag pool includes two re-entry warnings (BLDR, CRSP) that fail the higher re-entry bar, one justified fade candidate (CRSP), one sector-sympathy name with no company catalyst (ONTO), and one discretionary retail earnings miss (FIVE) that is correctly classified RATIONAL. Taking no conviction positions; one exploratory bet on CRSP as a sector-rotation fade.

entries.name: Builders FirstSource, Inc.
entries.catalyst: 5.02 officer change 8-K June 5 + housing/rate-sensitive sector rotation ahead of CPI
entries.thesis: BLDR up 6% on only 1.1x volume — note the lighter relative volume is notable. The 8-K is a single 5.02 item (officer change), which is rarely a 6% catalyst on its own. The move is more likely 80% sector rotation (housing/rates pre-CPI) + 20% reaction to whatever the 5.02 says. If CPI runs hot tomorrow, the rotation component reverses sharply. Fade setup into binary catalyst with stretched positioning.
entries.what_kills: Cool CPI extending rotation; or 8-K reveals a positive strategic appointment (new CEO with strong track record); or homebuilder peers continue to outperform
entries.reasoning: Re-entry warning applies: prior close 10 days ago for +3.18% gain on UNDERDONE thesis. New flag is OVERDONE conf 3 (fails re-entry confidence bar of ≥4). More critically: the new thesis rests on 'CPI binary tomorrow triggers rotation reversal' — this is not genuinely new information relative to exit reasoning (sector rotation was operative 10 days ago too). The 8-K catalyst (officer change 5.02) is dated June 5, and the flag was probably generated after the 6% move already printed. Sector rotation fades on CPI are legitimate, but a conf-3 re-entry on a recently-won position needs either higher confidence OR a material fact that wasn't known at the exit. Neither condition is met. Wait for CPI reaction clarity and better entry.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: Sector sympathy with Micron-led memory rally; no 8-K, no scheduled earnings
entries.thesis: Move is consistent with SMH +5% and the broader memory/semicap re-rating sparked by Micron commentary on long-term supply agreements. ONTO is leveraged to memory capex (HBM inspection), so it's a logical beneficiary. No company-specific catalyst — magnitude is roughly in line with sector beta given semicap names typically run ~1.2-1.5x SMH on up days.
entries.what_kills: If SMH gives back gains tomorrow and ONTO holds — would suggest a company-specific bid we missed (M&A, contract win)
entries.reasoning: RATIONAL classification, no company-specific catalyst, move (6.22%) is explained entirely by sector beta relative to SMH (+5%). Flag acknowledges 'no company-specific catalyst' and notes ONTO ran ~1.2-1.5x SMH beta on the day. This is a pure sector-sympathy play on memory-capex re-rating sparked by Micron commentary. Five-day move is 5.19% on volume_multiple 0.86 (below average), suggesting the rally is broad and not concentrated in ONTO. Entering at the same moment the sector thesis prints defeats the risk/reward: if memory thesis extends, you capture beta; if it reverses (likely scenario for any sector re-rating driven by guidance comments), ONTO gives it back. No asymmetric edge here. Conf 3 + no company driver + already-captured move = SKIP.

entries.name: CRISPR Therapeutics AG
entries.catalyst: 8-K filed 2026-06-04 with items 5.02, 5.03, 5.07, 9.01 — pattern consistent with annual meeting results
entries.thesis: 8-K items 5.02 + 5.03 + 5.07 dated 2026-06-04 fit the pattern of an annual meeting filing (5.07 = vote results, 5.03 = charter amendments approved, 5.02 = board reshuffle/director election). That's normally NOT a material negative — it's housekeeping. But on a risk-off day with XBI -3.6%, the market is selling first and reading later. If the 5.02 turns out to be a routine board election rather than a CEO/CSO departure, the magnitude (~3x XBI move) is excessive for a clinical-stage name with no pipeline news.
entries.what_kills: 5.02 turns out to be an unplanned CFO/CMO/CEO departure, or 5.03 includes a dilutive authorized-share increase that signals impending raise — either would justify the move and then some
entries.reasoning: Re-entry warning applies: you CLOSED CRSP 0 days ago (+0.28% gain) after flagging the exact same 8-K as OVERDONE, citing thesis conflict. The exit reasoning is sound: if 8-K items 5.02/5.03/5.07 are routine annual-meeting boilerplate (board election, charter amendments, vote results), the original UNDERDONE thesis holds and the -8.97% move is noise. If they contain an unplanned executive departure or dilutive authorization, the move is justified and the re-entry is moot. The new OVERDONE conf-3 flag argues the move is excessive (~3x XBI beta for a clinical-stage name), which is a legitimate fade setup — BUT: (1) confidence is only 3, failing the re-entry bar, and (2) you JUST exited because of this thesis ambiguity. The 8-K text needs to be read (are the 5.02 items election results or departure?). Once that ambiguity resolves tomorrow morning, CRSP will likely either bounce hard (if boilerplate) or hold weakness (if bad news). That clarity will give you a stronger re-entry signal than the current OVERDONE conf-3 classification. WATCH until 8-K details are public and market reprices.

entries.name: Five Below, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Q1 earnings release filed via 8-K on 2026-06-03 (item 2.02)
entries.thesis: A sharp double-digit decline on the day of an earnings 8-K, in the context of LULU also cutting guidance and citing undisclosed headwinds, fits a justified earnings disappointment in discretionary retail. The five-day move closely matches today's move, suggesting the drop is the earnings reaction itself rather than an overshoot. Magnitude looks appropriate for a guide-down at a stock that had been priced for continued execution.
entries.what_kills: 8-K reveals a beat with conservative guide that was misread; or a one-time charge (inventory write-down) that's non-recurring, in which case the move would be OVERDONE rather than RATIONAL.
entries.reasoning: RATIONAL classification, confidence 4, earnings-driven decline. The thesis is internally sound: -13.78% move on -14.55% five-day change + 3.57x volume is a justified earnings reaction. Magnitude aligns with a comp-store miss and/or guide cut in discretionary retail, especially in the context of LULU cutting guidance the same day. However, RATIONAL classification is explicitly not a buy signal in this framework — it means the price action is justified and proportional. Entering FIVE at the bottom of an earnings washout is a reversal bet against justified weakness. Your own track record shows 52% hit rate on conf-3 trades and 75% on conf-4, but those are OVERDONE/UNDERDONE classifications — RATIONAL is a different animal. RATIONAL means 'the market got it right on day 1,' which is the opposite of an edge. The five-day match (5-day -14.55% ≈ 1-day -13.78%) confirms the move is the earnings reaction itself, not an overshoot. SKIP: wait for reversal confirmation (bottom signal, stabilization) before entering.

entries.name: Veeco Instruments Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no news in feed
entries.thesis: VECO ripping ~10% on ~3x volume while SMH is down 1.2% is highly unusual and suggests a company-specific catalyst (analyst upgrade, contract win, M&A chatter, or industry conference comments) not visible in our 8-K/news feed. Without identifiable catalyst, direction of fade vs. follow-through cannot be called with conf 3+.
entries.what_kills: If the move is pure short-squeeze with no fundamental driver, fade likely; if a major contract is confirmed (e.g., advanced packaging for HBM), continuation likely

entries.name: Alaska Air Group, Inc.
entries.catalyst: 5.02 officer departure/appointment + 7.01 Reg FD disclosure June 3; possibly amplified by lower oil
entries.thesis: ALK up ~7% on ~1.8x volume following a 5.02 + 7.01 + 9.01 8-K from June 3. The combination of officer change PLUS Reg FD disclosure suggests management transition was accompanied by some forward-looking statement (possibly raised guidance or strategic update). Can't determine direction of news from item codes alone, and SKYW also up 5% suggests sector tailwind component. Sector strength + ambiguous filing = direction not callable at conf 3+.
entries.what_kills: If SKYW and other airlines are rallying equally on jet fuel/oil weakness (XLE -1.61%), this is sector sympathy not company-specific

entries.name: Pool Corporation
entries.catalyst: Apparent rotation into rate-sensitive / housing complex ahead of CPI
entries.thesis: POOL up ~6% on 1.7x volume with no 8-K and no news. Likely tied to the rotation into housing/rate-sensitive names (XLRE +2.13%) on softer rate expectations into tomorrow's CPI. BLDR also up 6% supports housing-rotation thesis. This is more of a thematic move than a stock-specific mispricing — UNCLEAR because magnitude (6%+) seems large for pure rotation absent a specific catalyst.
entries.what_kills: Hot CPI print tomorrow would unwind the rate-sensitive rotation and these names likely give back gains

entries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
entries.thesis: AVAV down 4.4% today and 13.6% over 5 days while ITA is up 1.4% and other defense names like DCO are up 4%. This is significant divergence from a strong sector. No 8-K, no news in feed — suggests possible secondary offering, contract loss, broker downgrade, or earnings positioning. Without catalyst visibility, cannot assess if oversold (UNDERDONE bounce) or just-starting (continuation down).
entries.what_kills: If AVAV reports earnings soon and is being de-risked into print, no mispricing — just positioning

entries.name: Masimo Corporation
entries.catalyst: Unknown — possibly institutional repositioning or M&A-related
entries.thesis: MASI closed essentially unchanged (+0.01%) on 3.14x normal volume. High volume + no price move = institutional rotation, block crossing, or a balanced fight between bulls and bears on a specific catalyst we can't see. This pattern often precedes a directional break once one side wins. No 8-K visible, no news. Worth flagging as something is happening here.
entries.what_kills: If volume returns to normal and price stays range-bound, this was just an isolated rebalance flow with no signal

entries.name: Lattice Semiconductor
entries.catalyst: Semi sector sympathy/catch-up trade; no company-specific signal
entries.thesis: LSCC was down 5-day before today and is closing the gap with the SMH rally. Move is consistent with short-covering on a low-volatility risk-on day rather than a fundamental catalyst — no 8-K, no earnings scheduled. The fact it underperformed the 5-day window while peers rallied suggests today is mean-reversion within the sector.
entries.what_kills: If it fades intraday tomorrow back to flat vs SMH — confirms one-day technical bounce

entries.name: Entegris, Inc.
entries.catalyst: AI/semi sector unwind; no company-specific catalyst found
entries.thesis: ENTG is high-beta to wafer-fab capex; with SMH down ~9% and ORCL/AVGO/NVDA all down hard on AI spend rerating, a double-digit drop in ENTG is in line with peers (VECO, ONTO, AEIS all down 7-8%). No company-specific 8-K, no earnings within window — this is sector beta, magnitude appropriate.
entries.what_kills: An 8-K appearing late showing a guide cut or customer-specific issue — would reframe this as company-specific and possibly UNDERDONE to the downside

entries.name: Texas Roadhouse, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Defensive rotation; no company-specific signal in catalyst feeds
entries.thesis: TXRH up on a day when XLY is down ~2% is the rotation tell — investors moving into defensive consumer names with steady traffic. No company-specific catalyst visible, but the relative move (TXRH vs. XLY ~8 points of dispersion) is consistent with classic risk-off rotation into staples-adjacent restaurants. Magnitude is appropriate to the regime shift; not a mispricing.
entries.what_kills: An undisclosed analyst upgrade or M&A rumor surfacing — would reframe as idiosyncratic and possibly UNDERDONE

entries.name: Intellia Therapeutics, Inc.
entries.thesis: NTLA leads the gene-editing group up double digits on elevated but not extreme volume, with empty catalyst_signals. Without an 8-K, a press release in news flow, or a conference data point I can point to, I cannot determine whether this is a clinical data leak, M&A speculation, or short-covering in a beaten-down name. Both a bull case (real catalyst, follow-through) and a bear case (squeeze fades) are plausible but neither articulates at conf 3+ without more evidence.
entries.what_kills: Move reverses tomorrow with no follow-through and no identified catalyst — confirms squeeze/noise.

entries.name: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions
entries.thesis: ITA is up nearly 3% and Kratos, AVAV, MRCY are all up mid-to-high single digits. The counterintuitive part: Trump-reluctant-to-resume-Iran-war should pressure defense, yet the group is bid. More likely explanation is a budget/appropriations catalyst, a specific drone/unmanned program award, or rotation into industrials (XLI +1.2%). With volume actually below average on KTOS and AVAV, this looks like a coordinated rotation/multi-day positioning rather than a news spike. Magnitude appears appropriate to a sector rotation move.
entries.what_kills: Stocks reverse with no identifiable catalyst — would suggest a one-day squeeze rather than rotation.

entries.name: GameStop Corporation
entries.thesis: GME up ~6% on 1.88x volume the day after an earnings 8-K. Cannot tell from filing codes alone whether print was actually good or whether this is retail/meme positioning. GME's price action has been decoupled from fundamentals for years; without reading the actual release, a directional call past 'reacted positively' would be guessing.

entries.name: Exelixis, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Healthcare/biotech sector strength; 5/29 8-K appears to be annual meeting (5.07) and director election (5.02), not today's driver
entries.thesis: EXEL +6.3% on below-average volume in a strong biotech tape. The 5.02 + 5.07 8-K is dated 5/29 (annual meeting director slate — routine), not today's catalyst. Move likely reflects XBI strength plus a possible clinical/pipeline data point not visible here. Magnitude is in line with quality biotech moving with a hot XBI day.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries
entries.thesis: AEIS up sharply but five-day still negative, suggesting today is a recovery within a downtrend driven by the broader semi-cap rally (SMH +4%). Magnitude reasonable given the bounce-back math and beta. No catalyst signals.
entries.what_kills: AEIS-specific announcement (guidance reset, customer concentration disclosure) emerging — would force reclassification

entries.name: PTC Therapeutics
entries.catalyst: Sympathy with XBI biotech selloff; no 8-K
entries.thesis: PTCT down hard but five-day is positive and volume is BELOW average (0.75x). XBI -4.4% explains roughly half the move, leaving a residual that could be idiosyncratic or simply beta-amplified. No 8-K, no earnings catalyst, no headline visible. Cannot articulate either a bull (oversold bounce) or bear (specific catalyst) case at conf 3+ without knowing what dragged XBI.

entries.name: Beam Therapeutics
entries.thesis: BEAM -7% on a day XBI is -4.4% is roughly the expected beta for a small-cap pre-revenue gene editing name. Volume is below average (0.87x), arguing against panic selling. Magnitude appears to match sector pressure rather than name-specific bad news.
entries.what_kills: An overnight 8-K on a pipeline setback or partnership termination would reclassify as UNDERDONE
